title: Message Batches API fan-out for Prometheus multi-category scans
source: Anthropic Message Batches API (~50% discount, async) — docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/batch-processing; live fetch blocked this run
description: A full Prometheus evolve cycle runs many independent scans (one per category, like this claude-features scan) that are not latency-sensitive. Running them as serial `claude -p` subprocesses is slower and full-price. The Message Batches API processes independent requests asynchronously at roughly half the token cost, ideal for the nightly/full-board evolve run.
proposed_changes: Add a batch execution path (SDK-based, since the CLI doesn't expose Batches) that submits all category scans of a full-board evolve cycle as one batch, polls for completion, and writes results into .cto/prometheus/proposals as they land. Keep the current per-scan subprocess path as the default/interactive mode and gate batching behind a `--batch` flag for non-interactive full-board runs. Record batch cost at the discounted rate in the ledger.
